prompt: A wide meadow at dusk photographed from a slightly elevated perspective./n/nAcross the grass stand several vertical piles of insect bodies arranged in a straight line across the landscape./n/n Each pile is composed of hundreds of small insects: moths, beetles, flies, and other species stacked naturally into small mounds./n/nThe piles form a clear histogram-like pattern./n/nOn the left side of the meadow the piles are tall and dense, representing large insect populations in the past./n/nMoving across the field from left to right, each pile becomes progressively smaller./n/nNear the right side of the meadow the piles shrink to only a few scattered insects./n/nAt the far right the ground is almost empty, with a single living insect resting on the grass./n/nThe descending sequence of piles clearly communicates a dramatic collapse of insect populations across time./n/nPhotorealistic macro ecological photography, cinematic natural dusk lighting, highly detailed insects and grass textures, shallow depth of field./n/nNo text, numbers, charts, labels, or symbols.s
dataset_name: A global database of long-term changes in insect assemblages
insight: A clear decline in insect populations across many study sites over time.
